zh.lifeSummary: 當Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland於1336年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾時，她的父親Robert II Stewart King of Scotland20歲，母親Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan16歲。她於1350年6月13日與John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1376年住在蘇格蘭阿吉爾可納達爾。她去世於1410年5月6日在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛哈林享年74歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾易納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland于1336年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾凱爾时，她的父亲Robert II Stewart King of Scotland20岁，母亲Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan16岁。她于1350年6月13日与John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles在苏格兰艾斯爾凱爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她于1376年住在苏格兰阿吉爾可納達爾。她去世于1410年5月6日在英国苏格兰阿吉爾洛哈林享年74岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾易納。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland was born in 1336, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, was 20 and her mother, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, was 16. She married John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles on 13 June 1350, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Knapdale, Argyll, Scotland in 1376. She died on 6 May 1410, in Lochaline, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in Iona, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland est née en 1336, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles le 13 juin 1350, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle a vécu à Knapdale, Argyll, Écosse en 1376. Elle est décédée le 6 mai 1410, à Lochaline, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Iona, Argyll, Écosse.

ja.lifeSummary: Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotlandが1336年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルで生まれた時，父Robert II Stewart King of Scotlandは20歳，母Elizabeth Mure of Rowallanは16歳であった。1350年6月13日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルでJohn Carrach MacDonald of the Islesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1376年にはスコットランドアルグイルナプダルに住んでいた。彼女は1410年5月6日にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャラインで74歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルアイオナにて埋葬された。

ko.Islay, Argyll, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 이슬레이
ko.lifeSummary: Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland(이)가 1336년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert II Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1350년 6월 13일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1376년도에 스컷랜드 아그이 냅데일에서 살았다. 그녀는 1410년 5월 6일 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭알라이인에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 이오나에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles em 13 de junho de 1350, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Knapdale, Argyll, Scotland em 1376. ru.Islay, Argyll, Scotland: ислеи, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland родилась в 1336 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert II Stewart King of Scotland было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles 13 июня 1350 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности напдеил, аргилл, Шотландия в 1376 году. Она умерла 6 мая 1410 г. в местности лочэлаин, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности иона, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.Duart Castle, Argyll, Scotland: Duart Castle, Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Princess Margaret Stewart of Scotland nació en 1336, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Robert II Stewart King of Scotland, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mure of Rowallan, tenía 16 años. Se casó con John Carrach MacDonald of the Isles el 13 de junio de 1350, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Knapdale, Argyll, Scotland en 1376. Murió el 6 de mayo de 1410, en Lochaline, Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Iona, Argyll, Scotland.
